stem: A mission enters the space mission pipeline. The probability of passing design review is 96%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing testing is 73%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing launch is between 72% and 91% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the mission is successful?

ground_truth.answer: P(successful) ranges from 0.505 to 0.638 as the launch pass rate varies from 72% to 91%. Point estimate: 0.568.

solution_steps: The mission must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the launch stage creates a 13.3 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the launch rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
